home.subtitle: A sacred abode where divine blessings flow eternally. Experience the spiritual heritage and timeless traditions of this ancient temple.
home.aboutPreview: Sri Kanugonda Raya Swami Temple stands as a beacon of devotion and spiritual heritage in the heart of Andhra Pradesh. With centuries of sacred traditions, this temple continues to be a pilgrimage destination for thousands of devotees seeking divine blessings.

about.title: About the Temple
about.historyContent:
Sri Kanugonda Raya Swami Temple is an ancient temple situated in the scenic Kanugonda village of Andhra Pradesh. The temple, dedicated to Lord Raya Swami, has been a centre of spiritual worship for centuries. The temple architecture reflects the Dravidian style with intricate carvings and a majestic gopuram that stands as a testament to the rich cultural heritage of the region.

The temple was established during the period of the great Vijayanagara Empire and has since been a pivotal spiritual centre. Renovated and expanded over the centuries by devoted kings and philanthropists, the temple today stands as a beautiful amalgamation of ancient tradition and timeless devotion.

Devotees from across Andhra Pradesh and neighbouring states visit this sacred temple to seek the blessings of Lord Raya Swami, particularly during the annual Brahmotsavam which draws thousands of pilgrims.
about.deityContent:
Lord Raya Swami, the presiding deity of this temple, is worshipped as a powerful manifestation of divine grace. The deity is believed to bestow prosperity, good health, and spiritual upliftment upon devotees who visit with sincere devotion.

The main idol is an awe-inspiring sculpted form, adorned with precious ornaments and garlands of fresh flowers daily. The sanctum sanctorum radiates an atmosphere of profound peace and divine energy that touches every visitor's heart.

Special significance is attached to the Friday and Saturday pujas, where the deity is adorned with special alankaram and offered elaborate naivedyam prepared with traditional recipes passed down through generations.
